---
title: "2 · Evaluation data"
---

Evaluation data are used to **score** the twin and are kept strictly separate from processing,
compared at the native resolution of the sensor. They fall into two kinds — genuinely independent
references, and model products that share part of our modelling chain — and the distinction between
them is the difference between validation and a consistency check.

## Independent references

Held out of the estimator entirely; the honest test of skill:

| Dataset | Source & access | Native res | Evaluates |
|---|---|---|---|
| SMAP L4 root-zone / L3 | NASA (`earthaccess`) | 9 km | soil moisture (gridded, native-scale) |
| **ERA5-Land** | ECMWF / CDS | ~9 km, hourly | soil moisture, runoff, **snow (SWE)** — an *independent* forcing+model chain |
| GRACE / GRACE-FO | NASA JPL (`earthaccess`) | ~300 km | **groundwater storage** anomaly (basin-scale, independent of wells) |
| NLDAS-2 soil moisture | NASA GES DISC (`earthaccess`) | ~12 km | soil moisture (gridded evaluation) |
| SNOTEL / SCAN / USCRN θ (held out) | NRCS AWDB; NOAA NCEI | point | soil-moisture phase (leave-one-station-out) |
| SNOTEL SWE (held out) | NRCS AWDB | point | the snow clock (melt-out timing) |
| USGS gauges (held out) | USGS NWIS | basin | **fluxes** — baseflow index, runoff coefficient |
| NWIS wells (held out) | USGS | point | GWL state — amplitude, phase, block-CV baseline |

Gridded layers (SMAP, ERA5-Land, NLDAS, GRACE) are compared **upscale-then-compare** at native scale
rather than as points, so a coarse product is never mistaken for a fine observation.

**ERA5-Land is the most complete independent cross-check** available here: it carries soil moisture,
surface + subsurface **runoff**, and **snow water equivalent** on one ~9 km hourly grid, produced by a
land-surface model (HTESSEL) forced by ERA5 — a *different* forcing and a *different* model family from
ours. It can therefore test all three faces of the budget at once — the vadose store, the flux
partition, and the snowmelt clock — where each of our other references tests only one. It is used as an
**evaluation** dataset and never as forcing, so the independence is preserved
([the roadmap treats ERA5-Land as evaluation-only](https://github.com/gaia-hazlab/gwl-space-time-smooth)).
GRACE is the only independent constraint on **groundwater storage** — coarse, but it does not depend on
the well network at all, so it checks the water-table trend where the wells are absent.

## Model / reanalysis evaluation datasets

Model products used as evaluation datasets — **MERRA-2** root-zone soil moisture (0.5°, incl. 2025),
and reanalysis soil-water fields such as TerraClimate's. Correlation against these is *inter-model
agreement*, not independent validation.

| Dataset | Source & access | Native res | Evaluates |
|---|---|---|---|
| MERRA-2 root-zone SM | NASA GES DISC (`earthaccess`), incl. 2025 | 0.5° | soil moisture (upscale-then-compare) |
| TerraClimate soil-water | Climatology Lab THREDDS | ~4 km | soil moisture (shared-forcing consistency) |

## Consistency check vs independent validation

The critical honesty point: a model that shares our **forcing and model family** cannot
independently validate us. When our soil moisture agrees with a reanalysis soil-water field at
domain-mean $r \approx 0.94$ but that field is driven by the *same* precipitation and the *same*
Thornthwaite–Mather balance, the agreement is a **consistency check** — it confirms a sane
implementation, not accuracy. A genuinely independent check requires observations *outside* the
forcing chain: the in-situ SNOTEL/USCRN comparison (pooled $r \approx 0.33$ against measured θ in the
sparse Cascades) reads far lower, exactly as an honest independent test should. Which class each
comparison belongs to is reported alongside every number in
[Soil-state evaluation](05-state-evaluation.qmd).
